recipe.title: TikTok Tortilla Wrap Hack (Crunchwrap Supreme Edition)

ingredients.name: large flour tortillas
ingredients.note: ~10 inch diameter; recipe prefers flour over corn

ingredients.name: Mexican cheese blend
ingredients.note: split between beef quadrant and cheese-only quadrant

steps.text: Brown ground beef in a frying pan over medium-high heat until cooked through; drain excess fat if needed.
steps.visualCue: maillard crumbles, steam, pan contact sizzle
steps.textureCue: loose grains turning juicy then drier as fat renders
steps.runwayRisk: ambiguous raw-to-cooked color if time is compressed

steps.text: Add chili powder, cumin, onion powder, garlic powder, smoked paprika, and cayenne; stir to coat. Season with salt and pepper. Set aside.
steps.visualCue: spice dust on hot beef, glossy seasoned coat
steps.textureCue: spices blooming, meat surface tacky with oil
steps.runwayRisk: spice color drift toward generic brown mud

steps.text: Lay a tortilla on a cutting board. Cut a straight line from the center of the tortilla to one edge (hinge cut for quadrants).
steps.visualCue: single radial slit, flat round tortilla
steps.textureCue: clean knife glide, flexible pliable tortilla
steps.runwayRisk: multiple slashes or taco fold instead of one radial cut

steps.text: Fill quadrants Crunchwrap style: shredded lettuce + tomatoes one quarter; sour cream + tortilla chips another; Mexican cheese blend + taco beef another; extra cheese alone on the last quarter.
steps.visualCue: four distinct topping zones separated by imaginary quarter lines radiating from center
steps.textureCue: wet sour cream versus dry chips versus loose beef

steps.text: Fold starting from bottom-left quadrant up over top-left; fold that stack over toward top-right; fold down toward bottom-right to form a neat compact layered triangle puck.
steps.visualCue: accordion quarter-fold stacking into triangular package
steps.textureCue: flexing tortilla laminating layers without burrito roll
steps.runwayRisk: rolled cigar wrap morph instead of TikTok quadrant fold

steps.text: Dry pan or lightly oiled skillet on medium heat until tortilla is crispy and cheese melts throughout, flipping once, about 3-4 minutes per side.
steps.visualCue: golden blistered tortilla, cheese ooze at seam, flip
steps.textureCue: crisp outer shell, molten cheese bridge, steam when cut
steps.runwayRisk: burnt tortilla or unscored flip tearing layers

assumptions: Hero visualization follows the site's Crunchwrap Supreme edition quadrant map (lettuce/tomato BL; sour cream + chips TL; beef + cheese TR; cheese-only BR relative to slit orientation as staged on Licorn boards).
Tortillas are modeled at ~25 cm (10 in) diameter, not miniature street-taco size. Negative constraints: not oversized wrap blanket, not miniature taco disc.
Skillet toast is interpreted as induction-friendly dry or minimal-oil sauté with a rigid turner flip; avoid cloth-assisted hot lifts.
Serves-six batch is editorial context only; plating shows one wedge plus optional second in background.
Quadrant chirality differences between tutorial diagrams and cooks are acceptable if four distinct fillings remain visibly separated before folding.
Image model gpt_image_2 remains available offline for quadrant-filled and sliced-hero reference plates when Seedance geometry drifts.
criticalTransformations: Single radial cut from geometric center of flour tortilla to one rim creates fold hinge; must not become multiple pie slices or a full diameter cut.
Four-quadrant ingredient zoning must remain recognizable through fold sequence or TikTok mechanic reads wrong.
Three-step folds (BL over TL, then toward TR, then down to BR) compress fillings into laminated triangle puck, distinct from cylindrical burrito rolling.
Dual-sided skillet crisping melts internal cheese network while blistering tortilla skin; flipping once preserves structural integrity.
Cut cross-section exposes horizontal strata: tortilla shell, lettuce/tomato freshness, crunchy chip layer embedded in dairy, spiced beef.
visualTextureOpportunities: Brittle tortilla chip snap and cascading crumb shards as ASMR bait before savory heat.
Ground beef crumble sizzle hitting hot metal with audible sear progression.
Spice snowfall on steaming beef with glossy oil sheen rebound.
Sour cream smear resisting chip embed with micro crunches stacking.
Shredded lettuce loft and glossy tomato wedges squeezing trapped juice on fold pressure.
Cheese avalanche separating lean beef mound from slick chips.
Tortilla flex fold with subtle whitening stress lines yet no ripping.
Hot pan landing hiss and cheese ooze creeping at puck seam.
Flip moment showing blister micro-bubbles transitioning to mahogany freckling.
Cross-section steam plume revealing molten stretch and brittle chip strata.
possibleHooks: Open on extreme macro chip fracture ASMR, then immediate payoff with sour cream + chip pairing teased.
Radial center cut POV that looks wrong until quadrants fill (pattern interrupt).
Four-color quadrant fill top-down satisfying symmetry before folding origami.
First sizzle of sealed triangle hitting iron with cheese leak tease.
Cheese-pull cross-section micro-climax with layered crunch audible.
promptPolicySources: Recipe2Video production defaults: style preset asmr_food; video model seedance2; reference image model gpt_image_2; vertical 1080:1920
Workspace rules: seedance-references.mdc (kitchen continuity pair, nine-reference cap, English Seedance prompts, ASMR-only audio, no speech or music in video prompts)
Workspace skills: recipe-ingest, seedance-workflow, asset-reference-system, food-physics-reference, tiktok-food-direction, suno-music-generation

clarifyingQuestions.question: Should the on-camera hero use flour tortillas only, or do you want a corn-tortilla variant that changes color, pliability, and blister pattern?
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Corn vs flour alters reference generation, fold physics, and crisping behavior for Seedance.

clarifyingQuestions.question: Confirm the on-set board orientation for the radial cut (which compass edge is slit) so quadrant-to-filling mapping matches the blog's Crunchwrap diagram for hand continuity.
clarifyingQuestions.reason: Chirality mistakes make the fold read incorrect even if ingredients are accurate.
